data.title: EpicQuest stock falls after pricing $3.7M offering
data.description:
• EpicQuest Education Group (NASDAQ:EEIQ) announced it has signed a deal with institutional investors to sell about 5.07 million shares (or pre-funded warrants) at $0.73 each in a registered direct offering.
  • The gross proceeds are expected to be around $3.7 million, the net proceeds from which will be used for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • EEIQ --25.6% premarket to $0.922
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ZIM Integrated Shipping warns on Turkish ban on Israeli vessels
data.description:
ZIM Integrated Shipping (NYSE:ZIM) disclosed Monday it is developing a mitigation plan to reduce the potential negative impacts of a new rule implemented by Turkey's government barring vessels that are owned, managed or operated by an entity related to Israel from berthing in Turkish ports.

The company said it received notice of the new rule from Turkish Port Authorities on August 22 and re-routed company-operated vessels that were scheduled to call to Turkish ports.

ZIM (NYSE:ZIM) warned the Turkish ban would negatively affect its financial and operational results if the new regulation remains unchanged, but it reaffirmed recently revised FY 2025 guidance of adjusted EBITDA of $1.8B-$2.2B and adjusted EBIT of $550M-$950M.

data.title: AbbVie to buy Gilgamesh’s depression program for up to $1.2B
data.description:
AbbVie (NYSE:ABBV) has agreed to acquire from Gilgamesh Pharmaceuticals its lead investigational candidate, Bretisilocin, for up to $1.2 billion.

The deal includes upfront payment plus development milestones.

Bretisilocin, a short-acting serotonin (5-HT)2A receptor agonist and 5-HT releaser, is currently in Phase 2 development for the treatment of patients with moderate-to-severe major depressive disorder (MDD).

Positive topline results from a Phase 2a trial of bretisilocin in MDD showed a clinically meaningful and statistically significant reduction in depressive symptom severity compared to a low-dose active comparator.

As part of the transaction, Gilgamesh will spin off a new entity that will operate under the name Gilgamesh Pharma Inc. to hold its employees and other programs, including its oral NMDA receptor antagonist blixeprodil (GM-1020), cardio-safe ibogaine analog, M1/M4 agonist program, and existing collaboration with AbbVie.

This transaction builds upon AbbVie and Gilgamesh's 2024 collaboration and option-to-license agreement to advance the development of next-generation therapies for the treatment of psychiatric disorders. This option-to-license remains in effect and will be transferred to Gilgamesh Pharma Inc. in connection with the spin-out.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Jackson Financial launches two fixed index annuities
data.description:
Jackson Financial (NYSE:JXN) said on Monday that its insurance unit has launched two fixed index annuities, Jackson Income Assurance℠ and Jackson Income Assurance℠ Advisory.

The new products provide protected lifetime income with opportunities for guaranteed withdrawal balance growth.

data.title: Safety Shot raises $30M in strategic financing
data.description:
• Safety Shot (NASDAQ:SHOTW) has priced a $30M financing, including $5M in cash and $25M in BONK tokens from BONK founding members.

  • All shares in the offering and private placement were priced at $0.46 each.

  • The financing is backed by strategic investors, including FalconX.

  • The company gains a direct stake in letsBONK.fun, securing recurring revenue and building a treasury of BONK tokens.

  • Safety Shot aims to become a public markets bridge to DeFi, with the deal closing expected around August 27, 2025.

  • Source: Press release

data.title: POET Technologies to voluntarily delist from TSX, maintains Nasdaq listing
data.description:
• POET Technologies (NASDAQ:POET) will proceed with the voluntary delisting of its common shares from the TSX Venture Exchange.
  • The delisting is expected to take effect at the close of trading on or about August 27, 2025.
  • POET’s shares will continue to trade on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol “POET.”
  • The company has received approval for the delisting and completed the necessary application process.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: QYOU Media launches up to $750,000 private placement of units
data.description:
QYOU Media (OTCQB:QYOUF) announced on Monday an up to $750,000 non-brokered private placement offering of up to 25M units priced at $0.03 per unit.

Each unit will be comprised of one share and three-quarters of one share purchase warrant.

Each warrant entitles the holder to purchase one share at $0.06 per share for a period of 24 months from the close of the offering.

Net proceeds will be used for the repayment of outstanding loans, payment of amounts due from the acquisition of Chatterbox Technologies, and working capital and general corporate purposes.

The offering is expected to close on or about September 5.

data.title: La Rosa Holdings regains compliance with Nasdaq continued listing requirements
data.description:
La Rosa Holdings (NASDAQ:LRHC) has received formal notification from Nasdaq that it has regained compliance with the Nasdaq Listing Rule.

La Rosa Holdings (NASDAQ:LRHC) is down nearly 8% premarket.

data.title: Bank7 renews 2-year stock repurchase program
data.description:
• Bank7 (NASDAQ:BSVN) has renewed its stock repurchase program for another two years.
  • The program authorizes the repurchase of up to 750,000 shares of common stock.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Alaska Air gains after Raymond James turns bullish on the second-half outlook
data.description:
Alaska Air Group (NYSE:ALK) traded higher in early action on Monday after Raymond James upgraded the airline stock to an Outperform rating after having it set at Market Perform.

Analyst Savanthi Syth said current demand and competitive capacity trends increase the conviction in the firm's earnings forecasts on Alaska Air Group (NYSE:ALK), as well as the attractiveness of the risk-reward profile. 

"While remaining concerned about the increased complexity of the business model, particularly the widebody aircraft exposure, we have gained a greater appreciation of Alaska’s measured approach to taking on this risk," updated Syth. 

Syth thinks the outcome at Spirit Airlines (OTC:SAVEQ) will be an important development for the U.S. airline industry, noting that Alaska (ALK) is already a beneficiary of the struggles at Spirit. "While competitive trends are largely benign across most U.S. airlines, it is particularly attractive at Alaska with competitive seats in its markets scheduled to be (2)/(3)% y/y in 3Q/4Q25 vs. 0/2% in 1Q/2Q25," highlighted Syth.

Shares of Alaska Air Group (ALK) moved 1.2% higher premarket on Monday to $60.20 after gaining 8.3% on Friday. The 52-week high for the airline stock is $78.08.
